page: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v6 is an Edsv6 family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 8 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance is equipped with 3780 GB of local NVMe storage and does not include any GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates solid performance across memory and processing workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1714.0 and a multi-core score of 11346.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 25302.46. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only cached small-block bandwidth of 1171.61 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths reach 100.42 GB/sec and 57.42 GB/sec, respectively. The system shows strong performance in text processing with a Geekbench score of 2399.0 and achieves 21.05 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it processes 135M model text generation at a strong rate of 262.06 tokens per second.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core and substantial 3780 GB local storage, the E64-16ds_v6 offers high resource density for memory-intensive applications. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive RAM footprints but do not scale linearly with high vCPU counts, avoiding the licensing or infrastructure costs of over-provisioning compute cores. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high 32.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor running at 4.2 GHz, it includes 3780 GB of local NVMe storage and lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks indicate average to strong performance, highlighted by a Geekbench text processing score of 2399.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 21.05 million operations per second. Uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 100.42 GB/sec. This configuration provides high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not require excessive compute scaling. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local NVMe storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and local NVMe storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB memory-to-core ratio
3780 GB of local NVMe storage for high-speed data access
Strong text processing and 21.05 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics
